decks.notes: This deck focuses on aggressively playing creatures like Territorial Kavu, Ragavan, and Scion of Draco, while using spells like Lightning Bolt and Stubborn Denial to control the board. Leyline Binding and Leyline of the Guildpact help to protect your creatures and disrupt your opponent's strategy. Consign to Memory provides card advantage, while Arena of Glory pumps up your creatures. Teferi, Time Raveler helps to control the tempo of the game. The mana base consists of fetch lands and a mix of shock lands and triomes to ensure you have the right colors of mana for your spells.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive plays to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes low-cost creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to apply pressure early in the game. Cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer provide additional threats and card advantage. Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge offer removal options while Elegant Parlor and Arena of Glory provide additional utility. The deck also includes some control elements like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Thraben Charm to handle opposing threats. Overall, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently to secure victory.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to take control of the board through removal spells like Fatal Push and Counterspell, while using cards like Cling to Dust and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes various powerful creatures like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Orcish Bowmasters to apply pressure and close out the game. Additionally, cards like Psychic Frog and Quantum Riddler provide card advantage and help maintain control of the game. Wan Shi Tong, Librarian and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student offer additional card draw and filtering to ensure the deck runs smoothly.

The main concept of this deck is to efficiently combo off without relying on traditional lands. The deck uses cards like Lotus Bloom and Whir of Invention to generate mana and find key combo pieces like Goblin Charbelcher. It also includes cards like Tameshi, Reality Architect and Hydroelectric Specimen to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up your own win condition. Counterspells and other disruption cards are used to protect the combo and buy time to assemble the necessary pieces. The goal is to effectively control the game and win through a powerful combo without the use of traditional lands.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful land cards and Amulet of Vigor to ramp and generate mana quickly, allowing for explosive plays with Primeval Titan and Scapeshift. The main goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with large, impactful creatures and land abilities. The deck also includes various utility lands and spells to support the primary combo strategy and provide additional options for different game situations. Overall, the deck aims to win the game through efficient land-based combos and overwhelming the opponent with powerful creatures.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride, while utilizing cards like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Goblin Bombardment to deal direct damage to the opponent. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls to support the aggressive strategy and maintain board presence. The inclusion of efficient removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm help keep the opponent's board in check. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and relentless assault.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts, specifically robots and equipment, to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle. With a mix of powerful artifact creatures and cards that can quickly generate mana or disrupt the opponent's strategy, the deck aims to establish a dominant board presence early on and maintain pressure throughout the game. By efficiently combining threats and utility cards, the deck seeks to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent in order to secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on fast-paced aggression using low-cost creatures and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It aims to control the board while dealing direct damage, taking advantage of cards that can provide card advantage and pump up creatures. The mana base allows for consistent access to both blue and red mana sources for casting spells. Overall, the deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creature damage and direct burn spells, maintaining pressure throughout the game.

This deck focuses on assembling UrzaTron lands to generate a large amount of mana quickly. It uses control elements such as Warping Wail and Karn, the Great Creator to disrupt opponents' strategies and protect its own win condition. The deck also includes powerful Eldrazi creatures like Glaring Fleshraker and Devourer of Destiny to apply pressure and close out games. Additionally, it utilizes artifacts like Mystic Forge and Chalice of the Void to further control the board and maintain its advantage.

This deck utilizes the UrzaTron lands to quickly generate large amounts of mana in order to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures and planeswalkers. It aims to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Karn, the Great Creator, while also presenting strong threats like Sire of Seven Deaths and Ugin, the Eye of the Storm. The deck's synergy between its lands and spells allows for a versatile and adaptable playstyle, making it a formidable force in the Modern format.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly build up an infect count on the opponent using creatures like Blighted Agent, Glistener Elf, and Inkmoth Nexus, and then finish them off with pump spells like Might of Old Krosa, Scale Up, and Mutagenic Growth. Protection is also provided through cards like Vines of Vastwood and Spell Pierce. The deck aims to outpace the opponent and win with infect damage before they have a chance to stabilize or recover. The inclusion of Boseiju, Who Endures provides added protection against counterspells.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to control the game through counterspells, removals, and card advantage. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan by countering important spells, removing threats from the board, and gaining incremental advantage through card draw and graveyard interaction. The deck utilizes a mix of efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and board wipes like Toxic Deluge, along with powerful creatures like Wan Shi Tong, Librarian to dominate the battlefield. With a mix of disruption, card advantage, and efficient threats, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent in a longer game.

This deck focuses on using Eldrazi cards to ramp up mana quickly and control the game by playing powerful spells and creatures. It aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while overwhelming them with big Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and World Breaker. Cards like Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble help to control the board, while Ugin, the Eye of the Storms provides another win condition. The deck also includes mana ramping cards like Utopia Sprawl and Talisman of Impulse to accelerate the Eldrazi cards into play.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures to pressure the opponent while disrupting their game plan with disruptive spells and effects. By utilizing a mix of efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and powerful removal spells like Solitude, the deck aims to quickly close out games before the opponent can stabilize. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Consign to Memory and Wrath of the Skies to deal with problematic threats, giving it both a proactive and reactive game plan. By efficiently using its resources and disrupting the opponent's strategy, the deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver its opponents.

This deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Mine) to generate massive amounts of mana quickly. It utilizes powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Thought-Knot Seer and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes control elements like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms to further disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to control the game using the UrzaTron lands and powerful creatures to overwhelm the opponent.

The deck focuses on a combination of aggressive creature plays and disruptive spells to control the board and win the game quickly. By utilizing powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd alongside removal spells like Solitude and Prismatic Ending, the deck aims to apply pressure on the opponent while stopping their strategies. Teferi, Time Raveler is used to disrupt the opponent's plays while cards like Consign to Memory and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker add versatility to the deck. The deck's objective is to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent to secure a victory.
